cycleContext.note: June 11 scan (14-day window: 2026-05-28–2026-06-11). Key signals: (1) Iran executions 124th week "No to Executions Tuesdays" — 40 prisoners including 19 protesters executed since US-Israeli attacks Feb 28; (2) DRC Ebola surges to 635/127 as of June 9 (+37 in one day); (3) Bolivia protests enter 10th week — military crackdown law signed; (4) Oracle WARN Act June 15 deadline passed — 30,000 workers forced to sign or forfeit; (5) Anthropic June 11 pledges $350M + Amodei demands government power to block unsafe models; (6) Sudan: RSF declared terrorists by Foreign Policy June 11; 28.9M in famine; (7) Myanmar: cluster munitions used in school attack June 9, 6 killed; (8) Florida: 39,000 immigration arrests in 416 days, 93/day, all 67 counties ICE-partnered; (9) North Korea: forced labor in EU supply chains documented June 5 (HRW); (10) Hormuz crisis disrupting global aid networks per UN News June 11.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.summary: Execution surge continues unabated. "No to Executions Tuesdays" enters 124th consecutive week across 56 prisons (June 9). Two Kurdish political prisoners Ashkan Maleki and Mehrdad Mohammadinia secretly executed June 1 at Ghezel Hesar Prison. Since US-Israeli airstrikes began Feb 28, at least 40 prisoners including 19 protesters executed on political charges — one every 49 hours. 784+ documented executions YTD. NCRI June 11 brief documents additional political prisoner executions. Iran accelerating capital punishment under cover of conflict per NPR.

top_entities.name: Bolivia
top_entities.summary: Protests enter 10th week. Military crackdown law (signed June 8 by President Paz) grants soldiers presumption-of-legality shield when acting against protesters. Death toll: 10 killed, 37 injured, 365+ arrested; terrorism charges filed against 25 union leaders. ILO and HRW condemn as violation of ILO Convention 87. Three deaths from hospital supply blockades. Bolivia now approaching constitutional threshold that historically triggers government collapse.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
top_entities.summary: Ebola Bundibugyo escalating: 635 confirmed / 127 deaths as of June 9 (up from 598/115 June 8; +37 cases, +12 deaths in one day). 18 health zones in Ituri affected including new zone Tchomia. North Kivu 32 cases; South Kivu 3. WHO PHEIC active since May 17. No approved vaccine for Bundibugyo strain. 260 hospitalized in isolation. $518M WHO/Africa CDC response plan active. 26.5M acutely hungry concurrently.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.summary: Gaza ceasefire violations ongoing: Israel attacked Gaza 218 of 243 ceasefire days. Since Oct 10 2025–June 9 2026: only 52,129 of 144,000 allocated trucks entered Gaza (36%). Feb 28 closure cut weekly aid from 4,200 to 590 trucks. Middle East Eye documents 3,076+ violations including 1,109 airstrikes/shelling. Oxfam: 100+ days of Gaza deliberately deprived of water. Netanyahu plan to seize 70% of Gaza territory advancing.

top_entities.name: Oracle Corporation
top_entities.summary: June 15 WARN Act sign-or-forfeit deadline passed. ~30,000 workers required to sign legal release waiving right to sue or forfeit severance. WARN Act 60-day pay period consumed all severance value. Oracle refused all negotiation (TechCrunch May 8). RSUs forfeited without acceleration. H-1B holders, cancer patients, pregnant workers disproportionately affected. State investigations active in WA and MO. Coworker.org petition ongoing. June: post-deadline coverage confirming workers forced to accept or walk.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.summary: DOJ criminal probe into Medicare Advantage risk-score inflation ongoing. Multiple coordinated state AG investigations announced June 2026. AI-powered claim denial practices under multiple federal/state cases. CEO Andrew Witty resigned; Stephen Hemsley returned. Shareholder lawsuit active. Vertical integration (UHC + Optum + Change Healthcare) creating structural conflicts of interest under scrutiny. Persistently elevated medical costs worsening governance crisis.

top_entities.name: India
top_entities.summary: June 10 Al Jazeera: Bengali Muslim expulsions stoking religious tensions in West Bengal. BJP government "detect, delete, deport" campaign — ~5,000 Bangladeshi citizens deported, many Indian Muslim citizens caught in sweep. USCIRF June 2026: India expelling Rohingya refugees and Muslim citizens. HRW: hundreds expelled without due process, coercion at gunpoint. UN condemns non-refoulement violations. FL state gov't building "holding centres" for Muslim migrants.

top_entities.name: Haiti
top_entities.summary: UN Secretary-General visited June 8 — "harrowing needs" declaration. 1.5M displaced (IOM record); Port-au-Prince IDPs exceeded 300,000 first time on record after May Cité Soleil violence. South-East Department (previously safe) now affected — 5,000+ newly displaced. ~30,000 fled homes in last 10 days. Atlantic hurricane season active, compounding risk. 110,000+ forced returns to Haiti in 2026 including unaccompanied children.

top_entities.name: Myanmar
top_entities.summary: June 9: Burmese military airstrike kills Karenni Christian youth leader (Mizzima). June 9: Cluster munitions used in school attack, Karen State — 6 killed. June 4: Hospital and former KIO office hit in Kachin State. Pattern: junta consistently targets civilian-protected sites with no military presence. Fortify Rights June 10 investigation (prior cycle): monastery airstrike killed 28 civilians in Bago Region March 5. 5.2M displaced.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.summary: June 11: Foreign Policy documents RSF as terrorists — systematic attacks on civilian targets, hospitals, displacement camps, agricultural infrastructure. 28.9M people acutely food insecure; more famine than rest of world combined. RSF committing "severe violations" in el-Fasher detention centres including ethnically motivated killings. 2026 $2.8B humanitarian appeal only 40% funded. UN labels Sudan "abandoned crisis." UAE arms supply continues per ongoing investigations.

top_entities.name: United Arab Emirates
top_entities.summary: New evidence of UAE fueling Sudan genocide: Refugees International June 8 report, Daily Kos June 8, Genocide Watch active documentation. GSSG (Abu Dhabi firm) training Colombian mercenaries for RSF. Novara Media May 29: British-backed UAE trains mercenaries helping militia committing genocide. CAAT UK analysis: UAE complicity with western arms supply chain. UN Feb 19: RSF attacks bear hallmarks of genocide. UAE denies all involvement.

top_entities.name: Uganda
top_entities.summary: Ebola Bundibugyo cross-border spread: confirmed cases in Kampala (pop. 3M+) and Wakiso. As of June 9, Uganda case count rising from 19 reported June 6. CDC Travel Advisory Level 2. Urban transmission risk in Kampala is primary concern — all cases traced to DRC travel but community transmission not yet ruled out. WHO/Uganda MOH joint contact-tracing and isolation protocols active.

top_entities.name: United States
top_entities.summary: June 11: Florida immigration arrests surged quietly — 39,000 immigrants arrested in 416 days of Trump admin vs 11,088 in prior 416 days; 93 daily arrests, all 67 Florida counties have 287(g) agreements with ICE. June 4: ICE ended post-release detainee death reporting. 20 ICE custody deaths publicized as of June 4. GAO June 9: 3 deaths at Fort Bliss Camp East Montana including homicide with destroyed evidence. ACLU: 40+ deaths since deportation campaign. HUD homelessness policy shift abandons "housing first." 22 states considering anti-homeless laws.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.summary: June 11: TechTimes — Dario Amodei demands government power to block unsafe models; Anthropic pledges $350M ($200M Economic Futures Research Fund + $150M national fellowship). Advanced AI Framework published calling for mandatory third-party safety testing and civil penalties tied to global revenue for catastrophic-risk models. IPO S-1 filed June 1 at ~$965B valuation. Great American AI Act discussion draft (June 4) would mandate semi-annual audits — directly affects Anthropic.

top_entities.name: North Korea
top_entities.summary: June 5: HRW — North Korea forced labor reaching EU supply chains. Global Rights Compliance June 4: 100,000+ North Koreans working in 40 countries; 21 workers interviewed in Russia report confiscated passports, 12–16 hour days, 364 days/year. Products entering EU supply chains. EU must publish high-risk product database by June 14. Workers' wages go directly to Kim Jong-un's nuclear program. Russia: 100,000-strong forced labor programme with cockroaches, surveillance, 16-hour days (NBC News).

entity_reviews.name: Oracle Corporation
entity_reviews.summary: June 15 WARN Act sign-or-forfeit deadline passed. ~30,000 workers required to sign legal release waiving right to sue or forfeit severance. WARN Act 60-day pay period consumed all severance value. Oracle refused all negotiation (TechCrunch May 8). RSUs forfeited without acceleration. H-1B holders, cancer patients, pregnant workers disproportionately affected. State investigations active in WA and MO. Coworker.org petition ongoing. June: post-deadline coverage confirming workers forced to accept or walk.
